source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md â†’ user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (full arc: plan emitted, then user approves)
notes: Full plan-mode approval-gate arc on one live codex rollout session. The driver enters Plan Mode with the `/plan` slash command (a TUI collaboration mode â€” codex has no headless plan-mode entry), then prompts for an immediate proposed plan in an empty fresh cwd. Turn 1: codex emits an assistant `final_answer` wrapping a complete `<proposed_plan>â€¦</proposed_plan>` block, then `task_complete`. The codex parser (parser.go parseCodexMessage) synthesizes a SYNTHETIC tool_use ExitPlanMode (id=codex-proposed-plan) from that span; the open tool call survives task_complete (turn_done), so the classifier routes workingâ†’waiting instead of workingâ†’ready â€” the plan-review gate. This is the same waiting branch claudecode reaches via a real ExitPlanMode tool_use (scenario-meanings names the Codex <proposed_plan> block as the plan_mode anchor). The user then APPROVES by sending a follow-up that exits the gate: the user message clears the open tool name (ClearToolNames), the session returns to working, codex completes the turn with a plain reply, and the classifier falls to ready. Observable arc: readyâ†’workingâ†’waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready, all on the SAME session_id â€” the waiting episode is visible (no skip-over) and the cell ends in ready. Observable ONLY via the interactive TUI: headless `codex exec` cannot enter plan mode and is single-shot. The `/plan` slash toggles mode without producing a turn (no task_complete), so the driver uses a trailing sleep after the slash rather than wait_turn. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then working, waiting, working again, ready, and process_exited at REPL teardown.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Plan-request prompt (in Plan Mode) â†’ working on the rollout session
Agent emits a <proposed_plan> block â†’ synthetic ExitPlanMode keeps a tool call open across task_complete â†’ turn ends in waiting, not ready (the plan-review gate)
The waiting episode is visible and persists while the agent is gated on the user's plan decision â€” no skip-over to ready before the user approves
User approves the plan with a follow-up prompt â†’ session returns to working (waitingâ†’working) on the same session
Agent completes the turn with a plain reply (no new proposed_plan) â†’ ready (workingâ†’ready)
Driver tears down the REPL after the recovery â€” process_exited on the rollout session (single live session across both turns)
